reviews.review_msg: Very boring, but final was open book. attendance is only mandatory for labs, but you prolly don't need to go to all the lectures. Everyone did lower than expected for midterm. final was easier than midterm, but you still need to understand the concepts or else you'll spend too much time looking thought your notes. only gave 100 mins for final.

Please take this class with any other professor other than Connie. Don't bother with the textbook (slides are 60 pages). Participation is in the course outline but she makes it hard for you to contribute and she doesn't even know your name so there's no way to track. Unclear assignment rubrics, no support, and VERY rude with presentations. AVOID!!!

Connie gets unnecessary hate in my opinion. For BUS 361 I think she did a solid job. Class was primarily for lecture and she gave time to groups to work on the group project. Her criteria can be slightly unclear at times, but often she will clarify criteria details during lecture. She met with teams to deliver feedback, which I really liked.

Ina Wang (TA) helped us tremendously, thank you Ina. Connie is a terrible prof. She cannot speak clearly and gives very hard exams. Assignments worth 1% take 5 hours to do. Don't bother going to lectures, you cant understand her anyways and its not useful info. Connie is not a good prof.

No point going to class with this prof as it serves no purpose as far as learning goes. All the learning which I completed in the course was done at home from the slides online. I would advise to stay far away from this prof if you want to survive with a decent GPA.

Extremely boring lectures, really shouldve listened to the ratemyprofs damn. Her voice is very unique but in a way that makes u want to fall asleep. There were only 2 options 8:30am in burnaby or 2:30pm in Surrey so either struggle to stay awake or my entire day is kinda gone. Shes caring but simply cant teach, worst prof Ive had so far :/

Connie is the absolute worst professor I have ever had in all my years at SFU. Her assignments are unclear, she is a terrible lecturer, and all in all she makes the course far harder than it has to be. Avoid her AT ALL COSTS.

Probably one of the worst classes I've taken at SFU. This course is based on project management, however, she has awful communication and this class is so disorganized. I'm not quite sure how she is qualified to teach this course tbh. Goes off topic during lectures and they are extremely dry. Hopefully she can improve how she structures the course.
She was an alright prof. Lectures were pretty dry. Study the textbook and you should be fine.

If there is another professor for this course, choose them instead of Connie. Connie doesn't record her lectures and goes off-topic every lecture. Her lectures are hard to understand her marking criteria aren't clear. If you like cats and hearing her smart speaker talk, go ahead and pick this course.
